summary:
Weekly Digest

Issues Found
- Overseer — CONFIRMED: total GitHub API auth failure. Every tool (all reads, search, file_issue) returns 401 "Bad credentials". Overseer is fully blind: can't read any of the 3 projects, can't check its own health, can't dedupe or file issues. Root cause: dead/expired/misconfigured GitHub token shared by all tools.
- ⚠️ Self-file failed — the write path is broken by the same 401, so this ticket could NOT be auto-created. Manual action needed: rotate the overseer's GitHub PAT and verify scopes (repo read on all 4 repos + issues write).
- crypto-trading / coachvision / ufc-dashboard — no project bugs confirmed; all data reads were blocked by the same 401, so nothing could be verified this cycle.

Top Enhancement Ideas (ranked)
1. Overseer — Startup auth health-check (effort: low, impact: high): validate the GitHub credential at startup and fail loudly / alert on a dead token, instead of silently blinding every review and project check like it did this week.

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: I'll start by reading through all the logs in parallel to get a complete picture.

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: All four read tools are returning 401 authentication errors, which means the overseer can't access anything through its read capabilities — a systemic failure worth reporting. Filing an issue might hit the same credential problem, but this…
timeline.text_full: All four read tools are returning 401 authentication errors, which means the overseer can't access anything through its read capabilities — a systemic failure worth reporting. Filing an issue might hit the same credential problem, but this is still a legitimate bug that needs to be documented, even if the tools for reporting it are also compromised.

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: The search is failing with a 401 error too, which means I can't check for duplicates before filing — but filing would also fail with the same authentication issue anyway. I'm considering attempting to file an issue against overseer itself …
timeline.text_full: The search is failing with a 401 error too, which means I can't check for duplicates before filing — but filing would also fail with the same authentication issue anyway. I'm considering attempting to file an issue against overseer itself to document the credential failure, since that's the most valuable action and would confirm whether the write operations are completely broken. I'm going to attempt filing the issue once to confirm the write path is also broken and document the situation, even though I couldn't search for duplicates first. The auth failure is so fundamental that writes almost certainly won't work either, and attempting this will give me a complete picture of what's broken.

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: The filing endpoint is also returning 401 errors, so the write functionality is completely blocked. I'll wrap up and document what I've found.

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: I should start by reviewing all the project statuses before diving into ideas.

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: The read tools are all failing with authentication errors, which points to a configuration issue that another agent should handle. Even though I can't access the actual data, I can still work with the tool descriptions to brainstorm ideas.…
timeline.text_full: The read tools are all failing with authentication errors, which points to a configuration issue that another agent should handle. Even though I can't access the actual data, I can still work with the tool descriptions to brainstorm ideas. The fact that credential validation is failing upfront is actually worth flagging as an enhancement—adding preflight checks would improve robustness. Now I'm moving through different projects to propose concrete enhancement ideas, starting with the crypto trading bot and a backtesting harness that replays historical price data. Across the other projects, I'm seeing opportunities like adding temporal segmentation and rep counting for coachvision, caching and change detection for the UFC scraper to reduce redundant processing, and several improvements to the overseer itself—credential validation before reads, a triage board for visualizing enhancement ideas by effort and impact, and unit tests with mocked fixtures. I'm aiming for a good mix of low-effort, high-impact ideas spread across all four projects.

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: Both reports point to the same root cause: a GitHub API authentication failure blocking all the tools. The bug-hunter couldn't even file the issue because the filing process itself is blocked by this credential problem. The idea agent's re…
timeline.text_full: Both reports point to the same root cause: a GitHub API authentication failure blocking all the tools. The bug-hunter couldn't even file the issue because the filing process itself is blocked by this credential problem. The idea agent's report got cut off before showing the actual ideas, but it was planning to ground them in project resilience, including specifically how the overseer should handle credential failures. The concrete recommendation emerging from both: the overseer needs a startup auth health-check that validates credentials and alerts on failure before attempting any operations. That's the actionable enhancement I can surface with confidence.

timeline.label: reasoning
timeline.text: The tool returned not_configured, which means the delivery failed due to a configuration issue I can't resolve. I've already made the required call, so I won't attempt it again—I'll just report what happened to the user.
